prompt: Using RFdiffusion, design a de novo parallel coiled-coil trimer (70–90 residues per chain) that stabilises the SNAP25 SNARE motif in the pre-fusion conformation of the SNARE complex for use as a structural biology tool to study synaptic vesicle fusion. The assembly should form a stable trimer with Tm > 75°C. outline a methodology for: (1) RFdiffusion symmetric backbone generation for a three-helix coiled-coil, (2) ProteinMPNN sequence design with inter-chain packing constraints, (3) structure validation via SEC-MALS and thermal denaturation. Include discussion of co-crystallisation strategies with SNARE complex components.
source_citation: Derived from uniprot (2024_05); target: SNAP25 SNARE motifs; organism: Homo sapiens; catalogue version v1.0
